data.title: NewLake Capital raised to Buy at Lucid on cannabis reform buzz
data.description:
NewLake Capital Partners (NLCP) shares gained 3.5% in Friday morning trading after Lucid Capital Markets upgraded the cannabis REIT to Buy from Neutral on the potential for cannabis reform in Washington D.C.

Analyst Craig Kucera pointed to a report from The Washington Post that U.S. President Donald Trump may soon direct his administration to follow through on a campaign promise to unlock the medical use of cannabis by reclassifying it as a Schedule III drug. 

Following a Dec. 10 meeting with leaders from the cannabis space, Trump is reportedly expected to sign an executive order to move cannabis into a less restrictive classification under the Controlled Substances Act. But a final decision has yet to be announced.

Rescheduling cannabis to Schedule III from Schedule I would greatly benefit cannabis cultivators and dispensaries, "as it would lift restrictions under IRS Section 290E which disallows tax deductions (such as operating expenses, including rent) in calculating taxable income," Kucera wrote in a note.

NewLake (NLCP) provides real estate capital to these state-licensed cannabis operators via sale-leaseback transactions and third-party purchases and funding for build-to-suit projects.

Lucid's Buy rate compares with the SA Quant system rating of Hold, the average SA analyst rating of Buy and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Strong Buy.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Short sellers increase bets against S&P 500 technology stocks in November
data.description:
Short sellers boosted their bets against the information technology sector in November. The sector that holds the highest weightage on the S&P had an average short interest of 1.88% in November, up from 1.70% in October. 

The S&P 500’s information technology sector (XLK) has grown more than 26% so far this year, compared to an over 17% rise in the broader benchmark index. 

Super Micro Computer (SMCI) was the most shorted stock in the sector in November,  while Microsoft (MSFT) took the spot as the least shorted stock. 

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Super Micro Computer (SMCI), ON Semiconductor (ON), and First Solar (FSLR) were the top three most shorted stocks in November. Short interest in SMCI rose to 16.86% in November from 14.98% at the end of October. 

While ON Semiconductor (ON) and First Solar (FSLR) had short interests of 9.91% and 9.84%, respectively. 

Microsoft (MSFT) was the least shorted stock in the sector last month, with a short interest of 0.85%, preceded by Apple (AAPL) and Amphenol (APH), with short interests of 0.88% and 0.92%, respectively. 

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

IT Services was the most shorted industry within the information technology sector, with 3.31% short interest in November, down from 3.43% in October.  

Technology Hardware, Storage, and Peripherals was the second most shorted industry, with 2.27% short interest as of November 30th. 

Communications Equipment was the least shorted industry in the sector this month, with a short interest of 1.37%.

data.title: Nasdaq takes a hit as Broadcom guidance concerns puts AI trade under pressure
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were lower on Friday, with the Nasdaq Composite retracting as the AI trade came under pressure dragging the index.

The teach-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last -1.3% in late morning trade, while the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.8%.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI), on the other hand, had advanced and continued making new highs reaching a top of 48,886.86 points, but retrieved and was last -0.1%.

Chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) -10.5% warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders. 

“The bull is just three years old, there’s still a massive Wall of Worry to climb, there’s plenty of cash on the sidelines, corporate earnings are better than analysts expected, the consumer is not as dire as many proclaimed, and the Fed is easing – not tightening. Plus, many sectors and stocks are well rested and ready to run,” said Robert Edwards, CIO at Edwards Asset Management.

“What a run the mega-cap growth shares have been on – impressive. But we think 2026 is the year the leadership broadens and the baton is passed. Mega-cap growth has carried the load, but about two handfuls of companies now represent nearly 40% of the index. It’s time to spread the money around. Dividend growers, value, industrials, smaller companies – all have been forgotten, and forgotten stocks, whose operations are strong, often provide the biggest surprise,” Edwards added.

Also, among the Nasdaq’s ((COMP:IND)) top decliners are Micron Technology (MU), Applovin (APP), Palantir (PLTR), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD).

On the economic side, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said that waiting on more data before lowering interest rates would have been more prudent. Also, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she would prefer a “slightly more” restrictive monetary policy.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.19%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.56%.

data.title: Trump Administration to more aggressively use private companies in cybersecurity fight: report
data.description:
• The Trump Administration is planning to more aggressively use private companies in its efforts to thwart cybersecurity threats, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • The administration is getting ready to unveil a new national cyber strategy that will be released by the Office of the National Cyber Director in the next few weeks, the news outlet added, citing multiple people familiar with the draft. It could allow private companies to impose certain restrictions or consequences on adversaries who go after telecommunications and infrastructure networks or negatively impact businesses with ransomware attacks.
  • The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Potential related companies: Palo Alto Networks (PANW), CrowdStrike (CRWD), Fortinet (FTNT), Zscaler (ZS), Okta (OKTA)

data.title: UBS stock rises after some Swiss lawmakers float compromise capital plan
data.description:
UBS Group (UBS) stock climbed 2.1% in U.S. trading on Friday after a media report of a compromise plan on capital requirements was suggested by a group of Swiss legislators.

The suggested plan would allow the bank to use more kinds of assets to meet capital requirements than the government-endorsed proposal, which has been criticized for being so strict that it would make Swiss banks non-competitive with other global banks.

Under the plan put forth by an influential group of Swiss lawmakers, UBS (UBS) would be able to use a type of junior debt called AT1 bonds, instead of equity, to meet some of the new capital requirements outlined by the Swiss government, Bloomberg News reported. They also suggested continuing to let the bank use its software and tax credits to count toward the capital requirement. The rule, though, would limit investment banking growth.

The new proposal, first reported in Swiss newspaper NZZ, could help break a political standoff between Switzerland's largest lender and the government, which is seeking to boost capital demands by as much as $26B at UBS, Bloomberg said.

The push for stricter bank capital requirements comes after the collapse of Credit Suisse three years ago, which suffered a liquidity crisis after years of missteps by the bank led customers to withdraw deposits and investors refused to provide more support to the company. That led the Swiss government to broker the sale of Credit Suisse to UBS (UBS) in March 2023.

A UBS spokesperson said, the "proposal goes in a more constructive direction than the extreme approach proposed by the Federal Council. However, Switzerland already has the most stringent capital requirement regime in the world."

Also, the ratcheting up in regulation in Switzerland is occurring as many other financial centers are easing or simplifying regulatory requirements, the bank said. "That would compromise the international competitiveness of the Swiss financial center. UBS continues to advocate for a strengthening of the regulatory framework with targeted, proportionate and internationally aligned measures," the spokesperson said.

Last month, the Financial Times reported that UBS Chair Colm Kelleher had been in private talks with U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to move its headquarters to the U.S. as a contingency plan if the Swiss government didn't ease its capital demands. However, the company subsequently stated it wants to continue to operate out of Switzerland.

data.title: EMA panel recommends Lilly's Mounjaro to children 10 years and older
data.description:
• The European Medicine Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use ("CHMP") is recommending that use of Eli Lilly's (LLY) type 2 diabetes medicine Mounjaro (tirzepatide) be extended to children as young as 10 years old.
  • The recommendation is for the drug along with diet and exercise.
  • In the EU, Mounjaro is currently approved for individuals 18 years and older.

data.title: EastGroup Properties raised to Buy-equivalent at Piper Sandler on improved tenant demand
data.description:
EastGroup Properties (EGP) was upgraded at Piper Sandler after the NAREIT semi-annual confab on improved tenant demand.

"EGP observed a noticeable pickup in the past two months as leasing decisions are slowly returning to normal," said analysts Alexander Goldfarb and Connor Mitchell.

"Activity picked up in 4Q25 with 454k sf as tenants are breathing once again with tariff fears subsiding and decision-making resuming on new and expansion space, which could give rise to increasing development starts, that would represent a reversal of 2024 and 2025 where management reduced their construction targets throughout both years," added the analysts in a research note.

"Importantly, EGP's tenants are driven by local economies, not foreign trade, which suggests this year's pause was more about general nervousness versus actual slowdowns, and thus there could be pent-up demand that benefits 2026 leasing, assuming the economy improves," added the note.

The investment bank upgraded the stock to Overweight from Neutral, with the price target increased to $220.00 from $185.00. Shares were 1.25% higher at $186.57 during morning trading.

Piper Sandler's rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts rating of Buy. Seeking Alpha authors grade the stock as Strong Buy, while the Quant Rating system sees the stock as Hold.

data.title: Katapult, The Aaron’s Company, and CCF to combine in all-stock transaction
data.description:
• Katapult (NASDAQ:KPLT) announced on Friday that it had entered into a definitive agreement with The Aaron’s company, and CCF Holdings to combine in an all-stock transaction.
  • Following the transaction, which is expected to close in the first half of 2026,  Katapult, Aaron’s, and CCF Holdings will continue to operate under their existing brand names.
  • The combined company, which will be called Katapult Holdings, Inc., will be headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
  • In the agreement terms, current Katapult stockholders will own 6% of the combined company on a fully diluted basis, and stakeholders of Aaron’s and CCF Holdings will own the remainder.
  • Aaron’s and CCF Holdings will be subsidiaries of Katapult, which is expected to continue trading on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol “KPLT.”
  • The management team of the new entity will comprise Cory Miller as the CEO and  Aaron's Russell Falkenstein as the CFO. The combined company’s board of directors will be comprised of nine directors, including Kyle Hanson as Executive Chair, Cory Miller, and Orlando Zayas. Most of the board directors will be independent.
  • The combined company is expected to have a stronger financial and operating model that includes:
    • more than $4B in pro forma LTM revenue as of Q3 2025;
    • approximately $450M in pro forma LTM Adjusted EBITDA as of Q3 2025 and operating scale that supports long-term double-digit Adjusted EBITDA margin potential;
    • a combined reach that includes more than 7M recently served customers;
    • a broad portfolio of recurring revenue streams; and
    • more attractive unit economics that support long-term profitability.

data.title: Schwab November core net new assets slide 9% M/M, climb 40% Y/Y
data.description:
Core net new assets brought to Charles Schwab (SCHW) totaled $40.4B in November, down 9% sequentially, but up 40% from a year earlier, the firm said on Friday.

Total client assets of $11.8T were virtually unchanged from October and advanced 15% from November 2024.

New brokerage accounts opened last month totaled 365K, down 15% M/M and up 2% Y/Y.

At the end of November, Schwab (SCHW) had 38,266 active brokerage accounts, up slightly from 38,145 in end-October and +6% Y/Y.

SCHW shares slid 2% in Friday morning trading.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: Cannabis stocks, AVGO, LULU, and more
data.description:
Stock futures exhibited mixed performance on Friday, capping a week dominated by the Federal Reserve’s latest 25-basis-point rate cut and its cautious guidance on how monetary policy could unfold in 2026.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Tilray (TLRY) +27% – Cannabis stocks rallied in the U.S. after multiple media reports said President Donald Trump is expected to push for reclassifying marijuana as a less dangerous drug. The move would shift cannabis from Schedule I to Schedule III, lowering its federal risk classification and acknowledging medical use. Peers also caught a bid, with Canopy Growth (CGC), SNDL (SNDL), Aurora Cannabis (ACB), and Cronos Group (CRON) all trading higher. Media reports indicated Trump recently discussed the rescheduling proposal with senior policymakers and industry executives, although the White House stressed that no final decision has been reached.
  • Rivian Automotive (RIVN) +16% - Shares jumped after analysts reassessed the EV maker following its AI & Autonomy Day, where the company unveiled an in-house autonomy chip, a Gen 3 compute system, a foundational “Large Driving Model” AI stack, and a new Rivian Assistant. Rivian also highlighted Universal Hands-Free driving across 3.5M+ miles of mapped North American roads via an Autonomy+ subscription, alongside plans to add LiDAR and radar on future models as it targets eventual Level 4 capability and potential robotaxi applications. Needham reiterated its Buy rating and raised its price target to $23, citing Rivian’s vertical integration and accelerating software-driven differentiation. Morgan Stanley, however, maintained its Underweight rating and $12 PT, warning that demand and data-capture limitations could hinder Rivian’s autonomy roadmap and profitability. Wells Fargo kept an Equal Weight rating, noting strong enthusiasm around Rivian’s technology and brand but emphasizing the company’s limited production history and narrow margin for execution risk.
  • lululemon athletica (LULU) +12% - Shares surged after the company raised its full-year forecast, posted a strong Q3, and boosted its stock buyback program by $1B. For FY2025, Lululemon now expects EPS of $12.92–$13.02, with the $12.97 midpoint topping the $12.89 consensus and up from the prior $12.77–$12.97 range. Revenue is projected at $10.96B–$11.05B, with the $11.01B midpoint exceeding the $10.96B consensus and above the earlier $10.85B–$11B outlook. The company also announced it has hired an executive search firm to identify its next CEO, as Calvin McDonald will step down at the end of January, while board chair Marti Morfitt immediately assumes an expanded role as executive chair.
  • InterContinental Hotels (IHG) +2% – Shares saw the biggest gain in four months after Jefferies upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold and lifted its price target to 11,400p from 8,700p. The analysts said hotels remain one of the more attractive pockets of opportunity amid increasingly limited growth across the broader consumer sector.

Biggest stock losers

  • Broadcom (AVGO) -8% – Shares dipped despite the company delivering a strong FQ4 and guidance ahead of expectations, powered by a 28% Y/Y revenue jump to a record $18B and a 74% surge in AI semiconductor sales. CEO Hock Tan highlighted continued momentum into Q1, with AI chip revenue expected to double Y/Y to $8.2B. The company guided for ~$19.1B in Q1 revenue and a 67% adjusted EBITDA margin, topping the $18.38B consensus. The company also raised its quarterly dividend by 10% to $0.65 per share. The stock, however, fell after CFO Kirsten Spears cautioned on near-term gross margin pressure due to a higher mix of AI revenue, noting Q1 margins could decline by roughly 100 bps sequentially.
  • Roblox (RBLX) -3% – Shares slipped after JPMorgan downgraded the stock to neutral from overweight, expecting the company to cool off next year amid softening user engagement and bookings momentum. Analysts led by Cory Carpenter noted that weekly engagement trackers show Roblox’s most viral games have passed their peak, with additional pressure likely from the upcoming facial age-estimation rollout in January and the Russia ban’s impact on user numbers. JPMorgan still forecasts ~20% bookings growth in 2026 but sees limited upside unless new breakout hits emerge or advertising meaningfully accelerates, neither of which the firm is counting on.
